untranslatable_reason: EADB (Eve & Adam Double Bottom) is a candlestick pattern requiring visual identification of specific price action sequences (two bottoms with a recovery between them). This cannot be expressed using only the allowed technical indicators without pattern recognition logic. The signal also references regime-dependent statistics (bull vs bear market performance) which require market regime classification not available in the indicator set. Suggested workaround: Implement EADB pattern detection as a custom indicator or use price action rules combined with support/resistance levels.
translation_notes: Attempted translation uses pivot points and ADX as proxies for pattern recognition and market regime, but this does not accurately capture the EADB pattern definition. The 40% average rise, 78% success rate in bull markets, and specific failure rates at 5%, 10%, and 15% thresholds cannot be operationalized without custom pattern detection. Entry based on breakout above pivot (UP direction) with bull market regime filter. Exit uses reversal below pivot per AUTHOR_SILENT guidance.
